master_row.title: Trawinistic D'Alembertian
master_row.canonical_statement: The Trawinistic d'Alembertian is the wave operator induced by the regularized inverse metric and the Trawinistic connection. It governs propagation on the singularly aware manifold in the same way the ordinary box operator governs wave evolution on regular space-time.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as a differential equation relating ∇, φ, g_, a, b, g.
master_row.dictionary: The Trawinistic d’Alembertian (^T) acting on a field () is given by (^T= g^ab^T_a^T_b), where (g^ab) is the regularised inverse metric and (^T) the Trawinistic connection.
master_row.encyclopedia: $_T$ extends wave propagation and field equations into the Trawinistic manifold by replacing ordinary derivatives with TZP-compatible covariant derivatives. Interpretive role: This operator is the canonical propagation engine for Trawinistic wave equations. It connects the singular geometry, the regularized curvature sector, and the derivative structure into one transport law.
